title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledges past criticisms of his left hand, linking struggles to a serious wrist injury.
Brown underwent major left wrist surgery in 2021 and reinjured it in 2023, impacting his performance.
Since recovering, Brown has improved his left-hand skills, achieving career-highs in points, rebounds, and assists.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo, former Red Sox broadcaster, returns to Boston with the San Diego Padres, his team since 2016.
Orsillo reflects on his time with NESN, calling it his 'dream job,' while fans show continued support.
NESN colleagues, including Jim Rice and Tom Caron, reunite with Orsillo during his visit to Fenway Park.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Alex Cora has used Roman Anthony as the designated hitter due to defensive improvement needs.
Roman Anthony delivered key hits against the Padres, but his defensive play remains in focus.
The Red Sox have a 2-6 record early in the season, with Anthony batting .231 so far.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early pitched four innings with four walks and three hits, allowing two runs against the Padres.
The Red Sox lost 3-2 to the Padres after Aroldis Chapman gave up the winning run in the ninth inning.
Early tied a Red Sox record with 39 strikeouts in his first six MLB starts, sharing the mark with Daisuke Matsuzaka.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0 at home, improving their 2026 season home record to 2-0.
Revolution head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card in the 34th minute for leaving the technical area.
Luca Langoni contributed a goal and an assist, leading MLS with five assists in the 2026 season.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas will resume hitting from a tee Monday after being shut down due to a rib injury.
Johan Oviedo to meet with his Tommy John surgeon after right elbow strain and decreased pitch velocity.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval nearing minor league rehab assignments after recovering from 2025 injuries.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, 22, a cancer survivor from Dedham, will run the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed with an aggressive thyroid cancer on her first day of sophomore year at college.
Mass General's treatment enabled her graduation from Harvard and continued participation in lacrosse.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise $10,000 supporting Boston Medical Center's mission.
Beattie highlights BMC's social workers, emphasizing their role in providing housing, care coordination, and stability for patients.
Training included early runs and fundraisers, contributing to BMC's ability to offer compassionate care regardless of ability to pay.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium on April 3, remaining without a win in the NWSL.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasizes patience and improving precision in the final third as key for Boston Legacy FC's offensive struggles.
Forward Amanda Gutierres, despite being a key player, is urged to focus more on goal-scoring positions to enhance attacking threat.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., 57, faces charges for embezzling client funds and unauthorized law practice, prosecutors say.
Rappa allegedly misused $660,000 from a client trust account after being suspended from law practice.
He was ordered to surrender his passport and not contact witnesses, with bail set at $2,500.
